intent: Make the Spec Explorer actionable: run drift/coverage/adopt from the tree and show per-capability health, instead of CLI-only
expectations: no new execution engine — actions dispatch existing commands to the AI
no workspace Python execution by the extension
row health is a boolean/count, not the full drift/coverage report
the turned-off empty state remains (adopt is its only action)
approach: Dispatch drift/coverage/adopt through the existing provider-agnostic executeSlashCommand path; compute row health natively in livingSpecsModel.ts (coverage via fs, drift via one time-bounded git call), best-effort so the tree never blocks or breaks; new livingSpecsCommands.ts module + package.json menus gated on existing context values

decisions.decision: actions dispatch to the AI session, never execute workspace Python
decisions.why: one-way dispatch is the architecture; isolation rule forbids depending on workspace .specify
decisions.rejected: child_process to drift.py with an output panel

decisions.decision: row health computed in TS mirroring the CLI rules
decisions.why: livingSpecsModel.ts exists precisely as the no-Python mirror; reuses its glob matcher
decisions.rejected: shelling to the Python scripts or mtime-based drift guesses

decisions.decision: async time-bounded health with silent fallback
decisions.why: the tree must render identically to today on any failure (no git, missing tiers)

decisions.decision: health cache keyed by spec path, cleared on refresh
decisions.why: one git call per capability per refresh, not per tree paint

decisions.decision: drift shown via icon warning-color + description dot
decisions.why: TreeItem has no colored-description API; icon color is the supported channel

plan.summary: 4 commands + menus + TS-native health; new livingSpecsCommands.ts beside the provider
plan.key_finding: provider already emits living-specs-capability contextValues and the view is gated on companion.installed — no new context keys needed

tasks.summary: 10 tasks across 7 phases: foundational model+manifest+commands, one thin phase per story, health last, docs+gate polish

implement.summary: 4 explorer commands + row health shipped; 13 new tests; docs in-change
implement.key_finding: item.capability was already on the nodes — dispatch scoping needed zero provider changes

tests: livingSpecsModel.test.ts::reports drift when a matched file changed
13 new cases across both test modules
manual: docs/sidebar.md + README + CHANGELOG updated in-change

T001.did: CapabilityHealth + readCapabilityHealth: coverage count (fs, CLI rule) + drift via injectable time-bounded git runner; match/exclude carried on ResolvedCapability

T002.did: 4 commands + view/title (refresh, adopt) + view/item/context (drift, coverage on capability nodes) + palette-hidden node commands

T003.did: livingSpecsCommands.ts registers 4 commands (scoped dispatch via executeSlashCommand, refresh fires provider); wired at activation; compile clean

T004.did: Capability nodes carry the name via item.capability (already emitted); drift dispatch tests: scoped, unscoped, non-capability gating

T006.did: Adopt dispatches bare command from view menu; refresh never dispatches to AI; 7/7 tests green

T007.did: Rows render health: covered/total + drift dot (warning color), tooltip lines, async getChildren with per-refresh health cache

T008.did: 6 health tests: coverage counting (CLI rule), drift via injected git runner, exempt/own-file filtering, never-reject fallback; fixed a leaked race timer found by jest

T009.did: Actions + row-health documented in docs/sidebar.md, README Spec Explorer paragraph, and root CHANGELOG entry

T010.did: Full gate: tsc clean + 1149/1149 jest; SC-001..005 validated

warnings: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> resolves differently under ts-jest vs tsc — typed loosely with a comment

concerns.note: jest flagged a leaked race timer in readCapabilityHealth — fixed with clearTimeout in finally; watch for the pattern in future Promise.race timeouts
